acceptance.name: acceptance
acceptance.fullDescription: Write acceptance criteria for stories. Use when defining done for user stories or creating test cases.

api-testing.name: api-testing
api-testing.fullDescription: Use when testing REST APIs, GraphQL endpoints, writing integration tests for backends, or setting up API test automation.

AssetManagement.name: AssetManagement
AssetManagement.fullDescription: Brand asset management with constraint enforcement. USE WHEN creating images, logos, icons, colors, typography, visual assets, or auditing brand consistency for any project.

brainstorming.name: brainstorming
brainstorming.fullDescription: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

catchup.name: catchup
catchup.fullDescription: Restore context at session start. Use when starting a new session, returning to work after a break, or needing to understand current project state quickly.

commit.name: commit
commit.fullDescription: Smart commit with pre-commit checks and documentation refresh. Use when ready to commit changes to ensure quality and documentation stay in sync.

completion-promise-loop.name: completion-promise-loop
completion-promise-loop.fullDescription: Use when a task has verifiable success criteria and may need multiple agent attempts to converge — orchestrates promise → dispatch → verify → refine with bounded iterations

CORE.name: CORE
CORE.fullDescription: Personal AI Infrastructure core. AUTO-LOADS at session start. USE WHEN any session begins OR user asks about identity, response format, contacts, stack preferences.

CreateSkill.name: CreateSkill
CreateSkill.fullDescription: Create and validate skills. USE WHEN create skill, new skill, skill structure, canonicalize. SkillSearch('createskill') for docs.

decision-record.name: decision-record
decision-record.fullDescription: Document significant technical decisions. Use when making architectural choices, technology selections, or establishing patterns that affect system design.

diagram-standards.name: diagram-standards
diagram-standards.fullDescription: Use when creating SVG diagrams for documentation — architecture diagrams, flow charts, data flow diagrams, sequence diagrams, or dashboard wireframes — ensures accessible, brand-consistent, version-controllable diagrams

dispatching-parallel-agents.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
dispatching-parallel-agents.fullDescription: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

docs-architecture.name: docs-architecture
docs-architecture.fullDescription: Use when structuring documentation for a project, product, or organization — choosing what to write, for whom, at what depth, identifying documentation gaps, or creating a documentation strategy from scratch

docs-hygiene.name: docs-hygiene
docs-hygiene.fullDescription: Use when adding, modifying, or reviewing skills — audits skill files for staleness, cross-reference integrity, and sanitization issues

estimate.name: estimate
estimate.fullDescription: Estimate complexity and effort for tickets or features. Use when sizing work for sprint planning, providing estimates to stakeholders, or breaking down large tasks.

executing-plans.name: executing-plans
executing-plans.fullDescription: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

finishing-a-development-branch.name: finishing-a-development-branch
finishing-a-development-branch.fullDescription: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

fixing-test-suites.name: fixing-test-suites
fixing-test-suites.fullDescription: Use when fixing failing tests, especially auth mocking issues in Flask/pytest or React/Jest service mocking patterns

hooks-authoring.name: hooks-authoring
hooks-authoring.fullDescription: Use when creating, modifying, or debugging Claude Code hooks — covers exit code semantics, event types, input parsing, registration, and testing

knowledge-accumulation.name: knowledge-accumulation
knowledge-accumulation.fullDescription: Capture and reuse institutional knowledge. Use when solving tricky bugs, making architectural decisions, discovering integration quirks, or establishing reusable patterns. Every problem solved once is solved forever.

pr-completion-loop.name: pr-completion-loop
pr-completion-loop.fullDescription: Use when implementation is done and you need to get from "code written" to "PR ready" — orchestrates check → fix → review → verify in a convergent loop with max 3 iterations

prd.name: prd
prd.fullDescription: Create Product Requirements Documents. Use when defining new features, documenting product decisions, or aligning stakeholders on scope.

prioritize.name: prioritize
prioritize.fullDescription: Prioritize work using RICE framework. Use when deciding what to work on next, ordering backlogs, or making trade-off decisions.

project-instructions.name: project-instructions
project-instructions.fullDescription: Use when creating project-level CLAUDE.md files, generating AGENTS.md for cross-tool compatibility, or setting up per-project agent instructions

raid.name: raid
raid.fullDescription: Track Risks, Assumptions, Issues, and Dependencies. Use when managing project risks, surfacing blockers, or maintaining project health visibility.

react-performance.name: react-performance
react-performance.fullDescription: React and Next.js performance optimization. Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code, optimizing bundle size, or fixing performance issues.

receiving-code-review.name: receiving-code-review
receiving-code-review.fullDescription: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

requesting-code-review.name: requesting-code-review
requesting-code-review.fullDescription: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

sprint.name: sprint
sprint.fullDescription: Sprint planning and management. Use when starting a sprint, reviewing capacity, or tracking sprint progress.

status.name: status
status.fullDescription: Generate project status reports. Use when preparing for standups, stakeholder updates, or documenting current project state.

stories.name: stories
stories.fullDescription: Create user stories from requirements. Use when breaking down features into implementable units or preparing backlog items.

subagent-driven-development.name: subagent-driven-development
subagent-driven-development.fullDescription: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

systematic-debugging.name: systematic-debugging
systematic-debugging.fullDescription: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

test-driven-development.name: test-driven-development
test-driven-development.fullDescription: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

test-patterns.name: test-patterns
test-patterns.fullDescription: Use when writing tests, organizing test suites, creating mocks/fixtures, or establishing testing conventions for a project.

ui-review.name: ui-review
ui-review.fullDescription: Audit UI code against 100+ best practices for accessibility, performance, and UX. Use when reviewing frontend code, checking accessibility, or auditing design implementation.

using-git-worktrees.name: using-git-worktrees
using-git-worktrees.fullDescription: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification

using-superpowers.name: using-superpowers
using-superpowers.fullDescription: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

verification-before-completion.name: verification-before-completion
verification-before-completion.fullDescription: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

writing-plans.name: writing-plans
writing-plans.fullDescription: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

writing-skills.name: writing-skills
writing-skills.fullDescription: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
